message:
fix: remove scroll-blocking touchmove handler on mobile

CRITICAL FIX: Mobile scrolling was completely broken due to the preventBounce
touchmove handler calling e.preventDefault() on ALL touch scroll events.

Root cause analysis:
- The handler checked if the TARGET element had overflow/scrollHeight
- When scrolling the page body, the target is usually a <div> or text node,
  NOT the scrollable body element itself
- This caused e.preventDefault() to fire on every touchmove, blocking scroll

Changes:
- Removed entire preventBounce touchmove listener and handler
- CSS overscroll-behavior-y: none on body already handles iOS bounce
- Kept preventDoubleTapZoom but added exception for INPUT/TEXTAREA elements
  to allow double-tap for text selection
- Removed unnecessary touchstart listeners that were doing nothing
- Simplified cleanup function

Impact:
- Mobile scrolling now works perfectly
- iOS elastic bounce still prevented via CSS
- Better UX on form inputs (can double-tap to select text)
- Reduced event listener overhead

This fixes the "mobile UI is a disaster" issue where basic scrolling was
completely non-functional on touch devices.
